meaning: vi.漫步,徘徊,迷路,迷失方向,离题
examples: You can't simply wander around squatting on other people's property.
He allowed his mind to wander.
Try not to let your mind wander.
We kicked our Waterloo to West End wander off at the Jubiloo on Southbank.
The lodge offers accommodation for birdwatchers, or you can wander its paths as a day visitor.
